theorems.name: str_len_eq_length
theorems.statement: theorem str_len_eq_length (s : String) : str_len s = s.length

theorems.name: str_len_empty
theorems.statement: theorem str_len_empty : str_len "" = 0

theorems.name: str_len_nonneg
theorems.statement: theorem str_len_nonneg (s : String) : str_len s ≥ 0

lean_translation:
def str_equal (a b : String) : Bool :=
  a == b

theorems.name: str_equal_refl
theorems.statement: theorem str_equal_refl (s : String) : str_equal s s = true

theorems.name: str_equal_comm
theorems.statement: theorem str_equal_comm (a b : String) : str_equal a b = str_equal b a

theorems.name: str_equal_iff
theorems.statement: theorem str_equal_iff (a b : String) : str_equal a b = true ↔ a = b

lean_translation:
def is_palindrome (s : String) : Bool :=
  s.data == s.data.reverse

theorems.name: is_palindrome_empty
theorems.statement: theorem is_palindrome_empty : is_palindrome "" = true

theorems.name: is_palindrome_single
theorems.statement: theorem is_palindrome_single (c : Char) : is_palindrome ⟨[c]⟩ = true

theorems.name: is_palindrome_reverse
theorems.statement: theorem is_palindrome_reverse (s : String) : is_palindrome s = true → is_palindrome ⟨s.data.reverse⟩ = true

lean_translation:
def count_char (s : String) (c : Char) : Nat :=
  s.data.filter (· == c) |>.length

theorems.name: count_char_empty
theorems.statement: theorem count_char_empty (c : Char) : count_char "" c = 0

theorems.name: count_char_le_len
theorems.statement: theorem count_char_le_len (s : String) (c : Char) : count_char s c ≤ str_len s

theorems.name: count_char_nonneg
theorems.statement: theorem count_char_nonneg (s : String) (c : Char) : count_char s c ≥ 0

theorems.name: to_upper_length
theorems.statement: theorem to_upper_length (s : String) : (to_upper s).length = s.length

theorems.name: to_upper_idempotent
theorems.statement: theorem to_upper_idempotent (s : String) : to_upper (to_upper s) = to_upper s

theorems.name: to_upper_empty
theorems.statement: theorem to_upper_empty : to_upper "" = ""

theorems.name: to_lower_length
theorems.statement: theorem to_lower_length (s : String) : (to_lower s).length = s.length

theorems.name: to_lower_idempotent
theorems.statement: theorem to_lower_idempotent (s : String) : to_lower (to_lower s) = to_lower s

theorems.name: to_lower_empty
theorems.statement: theorem to_lower_empty : to_lower "" = ""

lean_translation:
def char_is_digit (c : Char) : Bool :=
  '0' ≤ c && c ≤ '9'

theorems.name: char_is_digit_range
theorems.statement: theorem char_is_digit_range (c : Char) : char_is_digit c = true ↔ '0' ≤ c ∧ c ≤ '9'

theorems.name: char_is_digit_not_alpha
theorems.statement: theorem char_is_digit_not_alpha (c : Char) : char_is_digit c = true → char_is_alpha c = false

lean_translation:
def char_is_alpha (c : Char) : Bool :=
  ('a' ≤ c && c ≤ 'z') || ('A' ≤ c && c ≤ 'Z')

theorems.name: char_is_alpha_range
theorems.statement: theorem char_is_alpha_range (c : Char) : char_is_alpha c = true ↔ ('a' ≤ c ∧ c ≤ 'z') ∨ ('A' ≤ c ∧ c ≤ 'Z')

theorems.name: char_is_alpha_not_digit
theorems.statement: theorem char_is_alpha_not_digit (c : Char) : char_is_alpha c = true → char_is_digit c = false

tests:
def str_len(s):
    c=0
    for _ in s: c+=1
    return c
assert str_len('')==0
assert str_len('abc')==3

theorems.name: str_len_eq_length
theorems.statement: theorem str_len_eq_length (s : String) : str_len s = s.length

theorems.name: str_len_empty
theorems.statement: theorem str_len_empty : str_len "" = 0

theorems.name: str_len_nonneg
theorems.statement: theorem str_len_nonneg (s : String) : str_len s ≥ 0

lean_translation:
def str_equal (a b : String) : Bool :=
  a == b
tests:
def str_equal(a,b):
    if len(a)!=len(b): return False
    for i in range(len(a)):
        if a[i]!=b[i]: return False
    return True
assert str_equal('abc','abc')
assert not str_equal('abc','abd')

theorems.name: str_equal_refl
theorems.statement: theorem str_equal_refl (s : String) : str_equal s s = true

theorems.name: str_equal_comm
theorems.statement: theorem str_equal_comm (a b : String) : str_equal a b = str_equal b a

theorems.name: str_equal_iff
theorems.statement: theorem str_equal_iff (a b : String) : str_equal a b = true ↔ a = b

lean_translation:
def is_palindrome (s : String) : Bool :=
  s.data == s.data.reverse
tests:
def is_palindrome(s):
    n=len(s)
    for i in range(n//2):
        if s[i]!=s[n-1-i]: return False
    return True
assert is_palindrome('racecar')
assert not is_palindrome('hello')
assert is_palindrome('')

theorems.name: is_palindrome_empty
theorems.statement: theorem is_palindrome_empty : is_palindrome "" = true

theorems.name: is_palindrome_single
theorems.statement: theorem is_palindrome_single (c : Char) : is_palindrome ⟨[c]⟩ = true

theorems.name: is_palindrome_reverse
theorems.statement: theorem is_palindrome_reverse (s : String) : is_palindrome s = true → is_palindrome ⟨s.data.reverse⟩ = true

lean_translation:
def count_char (s : String) (c : Char) : Nat :=
  s.data.filter (· == c) |>.length
tests:
def count_char(s, c):
    n=0
    for ch in s:
        if ch==c: n+=1
    return n
assert count_char('hello','l')==2
assert count_char('hello','z')==0

theorems.name: count_char_empty
theorems.statement: theorem count_char_empty (c : Char) : count_char "" c = 0

theorems.name: count_char_le_len
theorems.statement: theorem count_char_le_len (s : String) (c : Char) : count_char s c ≤ str_len s

theorems.name: count_char_nonneg
theorems.statement: theorem count_char_nonneg (s : String) (c : Char) : count_char s c ≥ 0

theorems.name: to_upper_length
theorems.statement: theorem to_upper_length (s : String) : (to_upper s).length = s.length

theorems.name: to_upper_idempotent
theorems.statement: theorem to_upper_idempotent (s : String) : to_upper (to_upper s) = to_upper s

theorems.name: to_upper_empty
theorems.statement: theorem to_upper_empty : to_upper "" = ""

theorems.name: to_lower_length
theorems.statement: theorem to_lower_length (s : String) : (to_lower s).length = s.length

theorems.name: to_lower_idempotent
theorems.statement: theorem to_lower_idempotent (s : String) : to_lower (to_lower s) = to_lower s

theorems.name: to_lower_empty
theorems.statement: theorem to_lower_empty : to_lower "" = ""

source:
def char_is_digit(c: str) -> bool:
    return '0' <= c <= '9'
lean_translation:
def char_is_digit (c : Char) : Bool :=
  '0' ≤ c && c ≤ '9'
tests:
def char_is_digit(c):
    return '0'<=c<='9'
assert char_is_digit('5')
assert not char_is_digit('a')

theorems.name: char_is_digit_range
theorems.statement: theorem char_is_digit_range (c : Char) : char_is_digit c = true ↔ '0' ≤ c ∧ c ≤ '9'

theorems.name: char_is_digit_not_alpha
theorems.statement: theorem char_is_digit_not_alpha (c : Char) : char_is_digit c = true → char_is_alpha c = false

source:
def char_is_alpha(c: str) -> bool:
    return ('a' <= c <= 'z') or ('A' <= c <= 'Z')
lean_translation:
def char_is_alpha (c : Char) : Bool :=
  ('a' ≤ c && c ≤ 'z') || ('A' ≤ c && c ≤ 'Z')

theorems.name: char_is_alpha_range
theorems.statement: theorem char_is_alpha_range (c : Char) : char_is_alpha c = true ↔ ('a' ≤ c ∧ c ≤ 'z') ∨ ('A' ≤ c ∧ c ≤ 'Z')

theorems.name: char_is_alpha_not_digit
theorems.statement: theorem char_is_alpha_not_digit (c : Char) : char_is_alpha c = true → char_is_digit c = false
